text: The surgeon uses an intraoperative ultrasound to locate and demarcate the resection margins of a three-centimeter lesion in the right hepatic lobe. The ultrasound shows the lesion has no relationship with the principal portal pedicles and hepatic veins. The coronary ligament is cut, and an atypical hepatectomy of segments seven and eight is initiated under ultrasound guidance.
The surgeon performs parenchymal transection using a monopolar hook, bipolar forceps, and a vessel sealer. Hemlock clips are applied to the major portal and hepatic veins to achieve hemostasis.

The surgeon performs parenchymal transection using two intermittent intracorporeal Pringle maneuvers, each lasting fifteen minutes. During the hepatic resection, two main subsegmental portal pedicles and three hepatic veins are identified, clipped, and sectioned.

After achieving hemostasis, the surgical specimen is extracted through the fan and steel incision. The procedure continues with the rectal resection, involving repositioning robotic ports with a twelve millimeter and eight millimeter trocar. The robotic boom is adjusted, and a new target anatomy is established for an extended medial to lateral approach.

The surgeon dissects the area respecting the Girauda fascia, identifying the left ureter. The inferior mesenteric artery is dissected 1.5 cm from the aorta, clipped with Hemolock, and sectioned. The inferior mesenteric vein undergoes similar dissection, clipping, and sectioning. The dissection continues until the lower border of the pancreas is visible. An extended medial-to-lateral approach is used for splenic flexure mobilization, separating the superior surface of the pancreas from the retroperitoneum and opening the lesser sac.
The surgeon mobilizes the left colon by dissecting the Toldt fascia using monopolar scissors and a vessel sealer, separating the colon from the surrounding tissue.

The surgeon begins the PME by dissecting the posterior plane, followed by the right and left planes. The tumor is visible on the anterior surface of the hyrectum, and the mesorectum dissection is performed five centimeters distal to the rectal lesion.
